spec.overview: This program is responsible for opening, reading, and closing a cross-reference file. It handles potential ABEND conditions and displays I/O status information.
responsibilities: Read records sequentially from the cross-reference file.
Handle program termination with ABEND and display I/O status.

paragraphs.name: 1000-XREFFILE-GET-NEXT
paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph reads the next record from the cross-reference file.
paragraphs.java_method_hint: Implement a method to read the next record from a file.

paragraphs.name: 0000-XREFFILE-OPEN
paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph handles the opening of the cross-reference file.
paragraphs.java_method_hint: Implement a method to open a file.

paragraphs.name: 9000-XREFFILE-CLOSE
paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph handles the closing of the cross-reference file.
paragraphs.java_method_hint: Implement a method to close a file.

paragraphs.name: 9999-ABEND-PROGRAM
paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph initiates an abnormal program termination (ABEND).
paragraphs.java_method_hint: Implement a method to trigger a program abend.

paragraphs.name: 9910-DISPLAY-IO-STATUS
paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph displays the I/O status of the cross-reference file.
paragraphs.java_method_hint: Implement a method to display file I/O status.

migration_risks: The specific file dataset name and its associated I/O operations are not detailed in the provided artifacts, requiring careful investigation during migration.
The behavior and error handling associated with the `CEE3ABD` call need to be thoroughly understood and replicated in Java.
The copybook `CVACT03Y` contains data structures that must be accurately translated into Java classes or records.
